{"id":"smallhold-k9i4","title":"Delivery circuit breaker: per-domain failure tracking, 1-hour pause","description":"Track consecutive delivery failures per target domain in-memory (HashMap<String, CircuitBreakerState>). After 10 consecutive failures to a domain: mark that domain's circuit as 'open' for 1 hour — skip deliveries to that domain until the hour expires. After 1 hour: try one delivery as 'probe'; on success, close circuit; on failure, extend by another hour.

Test discipline:
- 10 consecutive failures to example.com → circuit opens, subsequent deliveries skipped for that domain
- After 1 hour, probe attempt sent
- Probe success → circuit closes, deliveries resume
- Different domain unaffected by example.com's circuit

Defensive programming:
- Circuit state is in-memory only — lost on restart (acceptable, conservative behavior: open circuit = don't deliver)
- Don't let circuit state HashMap grow unbounded — prune entries for domains with no recent activity after 24h","acceptance_criteria":"10 failures opens circuit; other domains unaffected; circuit closes on successful probe after 1h","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:36:16Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:36:16Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-70g","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:36:25Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
